title: Plump British Potato Pudding
description: A comforting, malty, chewy British potato pudding infused with Korean ssamjang and cooked in a pressure cooker for a flexitarian twist.
instructions: Peel and thinly slice 800g potatoes into 1/8 inch pieces; set aside.
Whisk together 200ml milk, 30g melted butter, 80g flour, 1 tsp baking powder, 1 tsp sea salt, and 1/2 tsp black pepper until smooth.
Stir 50g ssamjang into the batter until fully incorporated.
Place a layer of sliced potatoes at the bottom of a greased pressure-cooker pot.
Pour half of the batter over the potatoes, then add another layer of sliced potatoes.
Top with the remaining batter and sprinkle 10g chopped parsley over the surface.
Seal the pressure‑cooker lid, lock, and cook on high pressure for 15 minutes.
Allow the pressure to release naturally for 5 minutes, then quick‑release the rest.
Open the lid carefully, remove the pudding, let it rest 5 minutes, then slice and serve.
